ddls:
CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "full_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, admin) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was last updated */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user account (e.g., active, suspended) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department or organization of the user */,
  "affiliation" TEXT /* Affiliation of the user (e.g., university, company) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_runners" (
  "runner_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each job runner */,
  "runner_name" TEXT /* Name of the job runner */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the job runner */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the job runner was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the job runner was last updated */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the job runner (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the job runner software */,
  "last_heartbeat" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last heartbeat received from the job runner */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("runner_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "object_stores" (
  "store_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each object store */,
  "store_name" TEXT /* Name of the object store */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the object store */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the object store was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the object store was last updated */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the object store (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  "capacity" TEXT /* Total capacity of the object store in bytes */,
  "used_capacity" TEXT /* Used capacity of the object store in bytes */,
  "last_audit" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last audit of the object store */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("store_id")
)
